description: Trigger-accuracy and behavioural evals. Trigger evals check whether the skill should fire on given prompts. Behavioural evals reference the inline ## Tests section of SKILL.md.

evals.prompt: Our deal-intake pipeline runs through five stages (form, qualification call, comp pull, MAO calc, offer letter). We're getting 200 forms/day but only sending 40 offers/day. Where's the bottleneck?
expectations: Skill invokes Theory of Constraints procedure
Skill runs MVI gate before producing any constraint claim
Output includes ranked interventions in exploit/subordinate/elevate order

evals.prompt: Our team is convinced engineering bandwidth is the constraint. Is that actually true? We have throughput numbers for each stage.
expectations: Skill invokes anti-anchoring guard before accepting operator's named bottleneck
If independent location disagrees, output presents both options without silent pick

evals.prompt: Our MRR grew 15% per month for 9 months then flat for 6 despite increasing marketing spend. What's going on?
Should we hire two engineers, automate the legal step, or restructure the workflow to skip legal entirely?

behavioural_tests_reference: See ## Tests section in SKILL.md for the 7 acceptance test cases per spec §11.
